mc.!-~.name: mc.!-~
mc.!-~.description: The !-~ object functions just like the -~ object, but the inlet order is reversed.

mc.!/~.name: mc.!/~
mc.!/~.description: The !/~ object functions just like the /~ object, but the inlet order is reversed.

mc.!=~.name: mc.!=~
mc.!=~.digest: Not equal to, comparison of two signals  (multichannel)
mc.!=~.description: !=~ outputs a 1 signal when the left input is not-equal to the right input and a 0 when it is equal to the right input. The right input can be a signal or a float.

mc.%~.name: mc.%~
mc.%~.digest: Divide two signals, output the remainder  (multichannel)
mc.%~.description: %~ is a signal remainder operator. If signals are connected to both inlets, the left signal is divided by the right signal, and the remainder is output. If a signal is only connected to left inlet, it is divided to the argument or a float in the right inlet. Note that multiple signals in the same inlet add together automatically.

mc.*~.name: mc.*~
mc.*~.description: *~ is a signal multiplier-operator that outputs a signal which is the multiplication between two signals.

mc.+=~.name: mc.+=~
mc.+=~.description: +=~ adds all the values it receives. The result can grow very large, very fast.

mc.+~.name: mc.+~
mc.+~.description: Use the +~ object to add two signals together, or to add an offset value to a signal.

mc.-~.name: mc.-~
mc.-~.description: Use the -~ object to perform signal subtraction (to output a signal which is the difference between two signals).

mc./~.name: mc./~
mc./~.description: Use the /~ object is to multiply a signal coming into the left inlet by the reciprocal of either the initial argument or an int or float received in the right inlet.

mc.2d.wave~.name: mc.2d.wave~
mc.2d.wave~.description: 2d.wave~ is similar to wave~, but with an additional axis. A given ms range of an audio file will be divided into n rows. Y phase input will determine which row(s) will be used for playback. When the 2d.wave~ object is instantiated as mcs.2d.wave~ its outputs are combined into a single multichannel output but otherwise it functions identically to 2d.wave~.

mc.<=~.name: mc.<=~
mc.<=~.digest: Is less than or equal to, comparison of two signals  (multichannel)
mc.<=~.description: <=~ outputs a 1 signal when the left input is less-than or equal-to the right input and a 0 when it is less-than the right input. The right input can be a signal or a float.

mc.<~.name: mc.<~
mc.<~.digest: Is less than, comparison of two signals  (multichannel)
mc.<~.description: <~ outputs a 1 signal when the left input is less-than the right input and a 0 when it is greater-than or equal-to the right input. The right input can be a signal or a float.

mc.==~.name: mc.==~
mc.==~.digest: Is equal to, comparison of two signals  (multichannel)
mc.==~.description: ==~ outputs a 1 signal when the left input is equal to the right input and a 0 when it is not equal to the right input. The right input can be a signal or a float.

mc.>=~.name: mc.>=~
mc.>=~.digest: Is greater than or equal to, comparison of two signals  (multichannel)
mc.>=~.description: >=~ outputs a 1 signal when the left input is greater-than or equal-to the right input and a 0 when it is less-than the right input. The right input can be a signal or a float.

mc.>~.name: mc.>~
mc.>~.digest: Is greater than, comparison of two signals (multichannel)
mc.>~.description: >~ outputs a 1 signal when the left input is greater-than the right input and a 0 when it is less-than or equal-to the right input. The right input can be a signal or a float.

mc.abs~.name: mc.abs~
mc.abs~.description: Use the abs~ object to take any given signal and output only the absolute (non-negative) translation of that signal (i.e. a rectified waveform). All negative values in the input signal are converted to positive values in the output.

mc.acosh~.name: mc.acosh~
mc.acosh~.description: Use the acosh~ object to calculate and output a signal that is the hyperbolic arc-cosine function of each sample of the input signal.

mc.acos~.name: mc.acos~
mc.acos~.description: Use the acos~ object to calculate and output a signal that is the arc-cosine function of each sample of the input signal.

mc.adc~.name: mc.adc~
mc.adc~.description: The mc.adc~ ("analog-to-digital converter") object outputs a multichannel signal with audio from hardware input devices.

inlets.digest: start, stop Turn Audio On and Off

mc.adsr~.name: mc.adsr~
mc.adsr~.description: The adsr~ object is an Attack-Decay-Sustain-Release signal controllable by signals. In some situations, a combination of the function and line~ objects may be a useful alternative.

mc.allpass~.name: mc.allpass~
mc.allpass~.description: Use the allpass~ object to filter an input with an allpass filter. The allpass filter has a flat magnitude response but a complex phase response, typically delaying sharp transients.

mc.amxd~.name: mc.amxd~
mc.amxd~.description: Use the amxd~ object to load a Max for Live device and use it in MSP. When the amxd~ object is instantiated as mcs.amxd~ its audio inputs combined into a single multichannel input and its audio outputs are combined into a single multichannel output.

mc.apply~.name: mc.apply~
mc.apply~.digest: Apply a Function to a Multichannel Signal
mc.apply~.description: The mc.apply~ object accepts a breakpoint function in the same format as line~ and applies it over the space of channels of a multi-channel signal. Each channel contains a sample of the function evenly divided in the X dimension. In addition, mc.apply~ can store up to 64 functions, edited by mc.function, which you can recall and continuously interpolate using a signal input.

mc.asinh~.name: mc.asinh~
mc.asinh~.description: Use the asinh~ object to calculate and output a signal that is the hyperbolic arc-sine function of each sample of the input signal.

mc.asin~.name: mc.asin~
mc.asin~.description: Use the asin~ object to calculate and output a signal that is the arc-sine function of each sample of the input signal.

mc.assign.name: mc.assign
mc.assign.description: The mc.assign object assigns MC channels to incoming messages (numbers, lists, and symbols) according to patterns of channels and delays you define.

mc.atan2~.name: mc.atan2~
mc.atan2~.description:
Use the acosh~ object to take two given x and y values and output a signal which is their arc-tangent function, calculated as follows:
		
		Arc-tangent (y/x)

mc.atanh~.name: mc.atanh~
mc.atanh~.description: Use the atanh~ object to calculate and output a signal that is the hyperbolic arc-tangent function of each sample of the input signal.

mc.atan~.name: mc.atan~
mc.atan~.description: Use the atan~ object to calculate and output a signal that is the arc-tangent function of each sample of the input signal.

mc.atodb~.name: mc.atodb~
mc.atodb~.digest: Convert a linear value to a signal-rate deciBel value  (multichannel)
mc.atodb~.description: Use the atodb~ to convert a signal representing a linear value to a deciBel equivalent.

mc.average~.name: mc.average~
mc.average~.description: Use the average~ to perform bipolar, absolute, or rms averaging on any input signal.

mc.avg~.name: mc.avg~
mc.avg~.description: Use the avg~ object to keep track of the average (absolute) amplitude of the input signal received over a specified time interval.

mc.bands~.name: mc.bands~
mc.bands~.description:
mc.bands~ divides the spectrum of an audio signal into separate bands 
		with each band represented as it's own channel of audio.

mc.biquad~.name: mc.biquad~
mc.biquad~.description:
biquad~ implements a two-pole, two-zero filter using the following equation:
		
		
		y[n] = a0 * x[n] + a1 * x[n-1] + a2 * x[n-2] - b1 * y[n-1] - b2 * y[n-2]
		
		
		You can specify the coefficients a0, a1, a2, b1, and b2 as signals or floats (if you make the filter explode by making the b coefficients too high, you can recover (after lowering them) with the clear message, or by turning the audio on and off).

mc.bitand~.name: mc.bitand~
mc.bitand~.digest: Bitwise and-operation of floating point signals (multichannel)
mc.bitand~.description: Use the bitand~ object to perform a bitwise intersection (a bitwise "and") on two incoming floating-point signals as either raw 32-bit data or as integer values. The output is a floating-point signal composed of those bits which are 1 in both numbers.

arguments.name: bitmask
arguments.digest: Bitmask to be used by the

mc.bitnot~.name: mc.bitnot~
mc.bitnot~.digest: Bitwise inversion of a floating point signal (multichannel)
mc.bitnot~.description: Use the bitnot~ object to perform a bitwise inversion on an incoming floating-point signal as either raw 32-bit data or as an integer value. All bit values of 1 are set to 0, and vice versa.

arguments.name: operational-mode
arguments.digest: Processing mode for floating signal/floating-point values

mc.bitor~.name: mc.bitor~
mc.bitor~.digest: Bitwise or-operation of floating point signals (multichannel)
mc.bitor~.description: Use the bitor~ object to perform a bitwise "or" on two incoming floating-point signals as either raw 32-bit data or as integer values. The bits of both incoming signals are compared, and a 1 is output if either of the two bit values is 1. The output is a floating-point signal composed of the resulting bit pattern.

arguments.name: bitmask
arguments.digest: Bitmask to be used by the

arguments.name: operational-mode
arguments.digest: Processing mode for floating signal/floating-point values

mc.bitsafe~.name: mc.bitsafe~
mc.bitsafe~.digest: Replace NaN and infinite signal values with 0  (multichannel)
mc.bitsafe~.description: Use the bitsafe~ object to detect NaN (not a number) and +/- infinity values in an incoming signal and replace those occurences with zero. This is useful in conjunction with the bitwise operators, or any other situation where these values are possible.

mc.bitshift~.name: mc.bitshift~
mc.bitshift~.digest: Bit shifting for floating point signals  (multichannel)
mc.bitshift~.description: Use the bitshift~ object to perform bitwise operations on a floating point signal as bits or as an integer.

arguments.name: number-of-bits/direction-of-shift
arguments.digest: Number of bits to be shifted

arguments.name: operational-mode
arguments.digest: Processing mode for floating signal/floating-point values

mc.bitxor~.name: mc.bitxor~
mc.bitxor~.digest: Bitwise exclusive-or-operation of floating point signals (multichannel)
mc.bitxor~.description: Use the bitxor~ object to perform a bitwise "exclusive or" operation on two incoming floating-point signals as either raw 32-bit data or as integer values. The bits of both incoming signals are compared, and the corresponding output bit will be set to 1 if the two bit values are different, and 0 if the two values are the same. The output is a floating-point signal composed of the resulting bit pattern.

arguments.name: bitmask
arguments.digest: Bitmask to be used by the

arguments.name: operational-mode
arguments.digest: Processing mode for floating signal/floating-point values

mc.buffir~.name: mc.buffir~
mc.buffir~.description: Use buffir~ object when you need a finite impulse response (FIR) filter that convolves an input signal with samples from an input buffer.

arguments.name: read-duration
arguments.digest: Size of input buffer to use when filtering

mc.cartopol~.name: mc.cartopol~
mc.cartopol~.digest: Signal Cartesian to Polar coordinate conversion  (multichannel)
mc.cartopol~.description: Use the cartopol~ object to convert signal values representing cartesian coordinates to a signal composed of polar coordinates.

mc.cascade~.name: mc.cascade~
mc.cascade~.description: Use the cascade~ to filter an input signal using a series of biquad filters.

mc.cell.name: mc.cell
mc.cell.description: The mc.cell objects takes a <column row value> message from the left outlet of the jit.cellblock object and produces a corresponding setvalue message for use with MC objects to set a value for a specific channel. Since jit.cellblock column numbers are 0-relative and MC channel numbers are 1-relative, mc.cell adds 1 to the incoming column number.

mc.change~.name: mc.change~
mc.change~.description: Use the change object to monitor increase, decrease, or no change in a signal value.

mc.channelcount~.name: mc.channelcount~
mc.channelcount~.description: mc.channelcount~ outputs the number of channels in a multichannel signal connected to its inlet. Any message sent to its inlet triggers the channel count out its left outlet. A signal reports the channel count out the right outlet when the audio is turned on.

outlets.digest: Channel Count of Input as int
Channel Count of Input as signal

mc.chord~.name: mc.chord~
mc.chord~.digest: Store and Recall Signal Values Associated with an Index
mc.chord~.description: The mc.chord~ object is something like a version of the Max coll object that generates audio signals. You can store lists (which you can think of as chords) of numbers associated with a unique index, then recall your lists with messages or signals. When recalled, list values are assigned to channels in a multi-channel signal according to one of several allocation modes.

mc.click~.name: mc.click~
mc.click~.description: Use the click~ to create and output an impulse which can be used as an impulse-trigger.

mc.clip~.name: mc.clip~
mc.clip~.description: Use the clip~ to constrain input signals between two specified values.

mc.combine~.name: mc.combine~
mc.combine~.digest: Combine inputs into a multichannel signal
mc.combine~.description: The mc.combine~ object combines inputs (signals, multichannel signals, floats) into one multichannel signal. Unlike mc.pack~, every channel of an input multichannel signal will be included in the output.

mc.comb~.name: mc.comb~
mc.comb~.description:
Use the comb~ object to apply the classic comb filtering effect to an audio input. The comb~ object mixes the current input sample with earlier input and/or output samples, according to the formula:
		
		
		yn = axn + bxn-(DR/1000) + cyn-(DR/1000)
		
		
		where R is the sampling rate and D is a delay time in milliseconds.

mc.cosh~.name: mc.cosh~
mc.cosh~.description: Use the cosh~ object to calculate and output a signal that is the hyperbolic cosine function of each sample of the input signal.

mc.cosx~.name: mc.cosx~
mc.cosx~.description: Use the cosx~ object to calculate and output a signal that is the cosine function of each sample of the input signal. The cosx~ object is a true π based function - it varies from the cos~ object, whose output is based around a value range of 0. - 1.0 and is intended for use as a lookup table with the phasor~ object.

mc.cos~.name: mc.cos~
mc.cos~.description: Use the cos~ object to calculate and output a signal that is the cosine function of each sample of the input signal.

mc.count~.name: mc.count~
mc.count~.description: Use the count~ object count samples elapsed and thus drive processes tightly synchronized to the sample rate. It outputs a signal increasing by 1 for each sample elapsed. It can be set to loop, and can be used to drive objects such as index~ with sample accuracy.

inlets.digest: int, bang Start Count, stop Stops It

mc.cross~.name: mc.cross~
mc.cross~.description: Use the cross~ object as a pair of symmetrical low+high pass 3rd order filters with lowpass and highpass outlets you can use separately or in combination to form a crossover filter.

mc.curve~.name: mc.curve~
mc.curve~.description: Use the curve~ object to produce a signal that goes from an initial to target value over a specified time. It is similar to the line~ object, it produces non-linear ramps using a piecewise approximation of an exponential function.

mc.cycle~.name: mc.cycle~
mc.cycle~.description: Use the cycle~ object to generate a periodic waveform. The default waveform is one cycle of a cosine wave. You can also use the wave~ object, which offers additional flexibility though slightly less optimization.

mc.dac~.name: mc.dac~
mc.dac~.description: The mc.dac~ ("digital-to-analog converter") object sends its signal inputs to audio hardware. Double-click on an mc.dac~ to open the Audio Status window to configure audio settings and hardware.

mc.dbtoa~.name: mc.dbtoa~
mc.dbtoa~.digest: Convert a deciBel value to a linear value at signal rate  (multichannel)
mc.dbtoa~.description: dbtoa~ takes any given signal representing a deciBel value and outputs a signal which is a linear value conversion of the input.

mc.degrade~.name: mc.degrade~
mc.degrade~.description: degrade~ takes any given signal and reduces the sampling rate and bit-depth as specified/desired.

mc.deinterleave~.name: mc.deinterleave~
mc.deinterleave~.description: The mc.deinterleave~ object separates a multichannel signal into two or more deinterleaved multichannel signals.

mc.delay~.name: mc.delay~
mc.delay~.description: Use the delay~ object to delay a signal by a certain amount of time. The delay time can be specified in samples (determined by the sampling rate), or using the Max time format syntax for tempo-relative values.

mc.deltaclip~.name: mc.deltaclip~
mc.deltaclip~.description: deltaclip~ limits the change between samples in an incoming signal. It is similar to the clip~ object, but it limits amplitude changes with respect to slope rather than amplitude.

mc.delta~.name: mc.delta~
mc.delta~.description: delta~ outputs a signal which represents the differences between each incoming sample value in the input signal.

mc.downsamp~.name: mc.downsamp~
mc.downsamp~.description: downsamp~ samples and holds a signal received in the left inlet at a rate set by an argument to the object of the value received in the right inlet, expressed in samples. No interpolation of the output is performed.

mc.dup~.name: mc.dup~
mc.dup~.digest: Create a multichannel signal that duplicates a single-channel input
mc.dup~.description: The mc.dup~ object creates multichannel signals from a single-channel signals by duplicating the input across all channels of the output.

mc.edge~.name: mc.edge~
mc.edge~.description: Use the edge~ to detect zero to non-zero (and vice versa) signal transitions and report a bang out of one of its two outlets according to which direction the transition has occurred.

outlets.digest: (bang) Output on zero to non-zero transition
(bang) Output on non-zero to zero transition

mc.evolve~.name: mc.evolve~
mc.evolve~.digest: Generate a periodic multichannel function from breakpoint ranges
mc.evolve~.description: The mc.evolve~ object accepts breakpoints consisting of ranges where both the domain and range go from 0 to 1. It maps this set of ranges across the space of a multichannel output signal, where the first channel in the output signal outputs the low end of the range and the last channel outputs the high end. Connect a phasor~ or another time-varying signal to mc.evolve~ to drive the output.

outlets.digest: Query value for an output channel
Query value of the output range

mc.ezadc~.name: mc.ezadc~
mc.ezadc~.description: mc.ezadc~ works as a user interface version of the mc.adc~ object. It appears as a button which can be clicked with the mouse to turn audio on or off.

mc.ezdac~.name: mc.ezdac~
mc.ezdac~.description: mc.ezdac~ works as a user interface version of the mc.dac~ object. It appears as a button which can be clicked with the mouse to turn audio on or off.

mc.fffb~.name: mc.fffb~
mc.fffb~.description: The fffb~ object implements a bank of bandpass filter objects, each of which is similar to the reson~ filter object. An input signal is applied to all filters, and the outputs of each filter are available separately. When the fffb~ object is instantiated as mcs.fffb~ the object has a single multichannel output containing the individual filters. Otherwise it has a separate outlet for each filter.

inlets.digest: signal input, lists for setting parameters

mc.fft~.name: mc.fft~
mc.fft~.description: fft~ performs a Fast Fourier transform on any incoming signal and outputs the real and imaginary parts of that transform as well as a synchronization signal.

outlets.digest: (signal) Ramp from 0 to Number of Points - 1

arguments.name: interval
arguments.digest: Number of samples between successive FFTs

mc.filtercoeff~.name: mc.filtercoeff~
mc.filtercoeff~.description: The filtercoeff~ object is a signal-rate filter coefficient calculator for the biquad~ object. It calculates the filter coefficients from three higher-level parameters: frequency, amplitude and resonance (Q) or slope (S). Its internal calculations are based on those of the filtergraph~ object.

mc.frameaccum~.name: mc.frameaccum~
mc.frameaccum~.digest: Compute "running phase" of successive phase deviation frames  (multichannel)
mc.frameaccum~.description: frameaccum~ computes a running phase by keeping a sum of the values in each position of its incoming signal vectors. When used inside a pfft~ object, it can keep a running phase of the FFT because the FFT size is equal to the signal vector size.

mc.frameaverage~.name: mc.frameaverage~
mc.frameaverage~.digest: Perform a piece-wise running averaging of consecutive frames of audio.  (multichannel)
mc.frameaverage~.description: The frameaverage~ object performs a piece-wise running averaging of consecutive frames of audio. Where the framesmooth~ object can be seen as smoothing in the frequency domain (when using FFT as input), the frameaverage~ object can be seen as performing temporal smoothing, more analogous to the vectral~ object.

mc.framedelta~.name: mc.framedelta~
mc.framedelta~.digest: Compute phase deviation between successive FFT frames  (multichannel)
mc.framedelta~.description: framedelta~ computes a running phase deviation by subtracting values in each position of its previously received signal vector from the current signal vector. When used inside a pfft~ object, it keeps a running phase deviation of the FFT because the FFT size is equal to the signal vector size.

mc.framesmooth~.name: mc.framesmooth~
mc.framesmooth~.digest: Perform averaging of consecutive samples, grouped into frames, without blurring across the frames. (multichannel)
mc.framesmooth~.description: The framesmooth~ object performs averaging of consecutive samples, grouped into frames, without blurring across the frames.

mc.freqshift~.name: mc.freqshift~
mc.freqshift~.description: freqshift~ is a time-domain frequency shifter (also known as a single-sideband ring modulator).

mc.ftom~.name: mc.ftom~
mc.ftom~.digest: Convert frequency to MIDI note numbers at signal-rate (multichannel)
mc.ftom~.description: Use ftom~ to convert frequency to MIDI note numbers at signal rate

mc.function.name: mc.function
mc.function.description: Draw or store a set of x, y points as floating-point numbers. The output the entire function is useful as an input for line~. You can also get an interpolated y value for any x value.

inlets.digest: bang Outputs All, float Outputs Y at X, list Edits

mc.fzero~.name: mc.fzero~
mc.fzero~.description: The fzero~ object estimates the fundamental frequency of an an incoming, monophonic audio signal. It performs multiple layers of wavelet transforms on an incoming vector, comparing the spacing between the peaks in each.

mc.gain~.name: mc.gain~
mc.gain~.description: The mc.gain~ object is a slider that scales multichannel signals. It can also make a smooth transition as you move from one value of the slider to the next.

inlets.digest: Input to Scale, int Sets Value

mc.gate~.name: mc.gate~
mc.gate~.digest: Route a signal to one of several outlets (multichannel)
mc.gate~.description:
Use gate~ to route an input signal at the second inlet to one of several outlets, or to no outlet at all. When there is only one outlet (the default case), it acts as a simple switch. Unlike the Max gate object, any outlet which is not selected outputs a signal composed of zero values.
		
		When the gate~ object is created as mcs.gate~ all of its signal outlets are combined into a single multichannel outlet. The behavior of mcs.gate~ is otherwise identical to gate~.

inlets.digest: int/signal Turns Input Off or Routes to Output

mc.gen.name: mc.gen
mc.gen.digest: Generate native audio event processing routines (multichannel)
mc.gen.description: The gen object turns an embedded Gen patcher into event processing routines analagous to what the gen~ object does for audio signals. The Gen patcher can be built from a wide set of low-level Gen operator objects as well as embedded code expressions.

arguments.name: patcher-name
arguments.digest: Name of a patcher to be loaded

mc.generate~.name: mc.generate~
mc.generate~.digest: Generate Values for a Range of Channels
mc.generate~.description: The mc.generate~ object allows signal-rate updating of MC Wrapper features such as deviate, spread, and harmonic as a trigger signal or parameter inputs change. One wrapper message is assigned as an operator.

mc.gen~.name: mc.gen~
mc.gen~.digest: Generate native audio signal processing routines (multichannel)
mc.gen~.description: The gen~ object turns an embedded Gen patcher into signal processing routines of optimized native machine code. The Gen patcher can be built from a wide set of low-level Gen operator objects as well as embedded code expressions.

arguments.name: patcher-name
arguments.digest: Name of a patcher to be loaded

mc.getattr.name: mc.getattr
mc.getattr.description: Provides a user interface to query attribute values from an object. You can also retrieve a list of all available attributes for the attached object.

mc.gradient~.name: mc.gradient~
mc.gradient~.digest: Generate a time-varying function over the space of a multichannel signal
mc.gradient~.description: Add breakpoints to the mc.gradient~ to specify a function where both the domain and range go from 0 to 1. The output range is mapped across the space of a multichannel signal. Connect a phasor~ or another time-varying single- or multichannel signal to the mc.gradient~ object to drive its output.

outlets.digest: Channel value in response to chanval message

mc.groove~.name: mc.groove~
mc.groove~.description: The groove~ object is a variable-rate, looping, sample-playback object which references the audio information stored in a buffer~ object having the same name. When the groove~ object is instantiated as mcs.groove~ its audio outputs are combined into a single multichannel output.

mc.hilbert~.name: mc.hilbert~
mc.hilbert~.description: hilbert~ is a 6th order hilbert transformer with a minimum of error.

mc.ifft~.name: mc.ifft~
mc.ifft~.description: ifft~ performs an Inverse FFT (inverse fast Fourier transform) on an input signal.

outlets.digest: (signal) Ramp from 0 to Number of Points - 1

arguments.name: interval
arguments.digest: Number of samples between successive IFFTs

mc.index~.name: mc.index~
mc.index~.description: Use index~ to read from a buffer~ object at a signal-driven sample index with no interpolation on the output.

mc.interleave~.name: mc.interleave~
mc.interleave~.digest: Interleave two or more multichannel signals
mc.interleave~.description: The mc.interleave~ object combines two or more multichannel signals into a single multichannel signal by interleaving channels. This is useful for re-combining stereo pairs from MC sample playback objects prior to sending to mc.mixdown~ or mc.stereo~.

mc.in~.name: mc.in~
mc.in~.digest: Signal inputs for a patcher loaded by poly~
mc.in~.description: The mc.in~ object receives signals from multiple inlets to the poly~ object (or multiple channels within the input to mcs.poly~), and outputs those signals as one multichannel signal. The number of signal inlets is determined by the chans attribute.

mc.jit.peek~.name: mc.jit.peek~
mc.jit.peek~.digest: Read matrix data as an audio signal  (multichannel)
mc.jit.peek~.description: jit.peek~ reads the value of one plane of a matrix cell at the position specified by the signal inputs. This value is output as a signal. The object arguments are [matrix_name]  [dim_inputcount]  [plane].

mc.kink~.name: mc.kink~
mc.kink~.description: kink~ takes phasor~ values and distorts them according to a slope factor. If the input times the slope is less than 0.5, that value is output. Otherwise, a complentary slope is used, equal to 0.5 at the same input value but equal to 1 when the input is 1. This creates a bend or "kink" in the phase waveform when the slope is not equal to 1.

mc.limi~.name: mc.limi~
mc.limi~.category: audio, effects, limiter, compressor, dynamics, Multichannel

arguments.name: channel_count
arguments.digest: The number of channels to process

arguments.name: buffer_size
arguments.digest: The size (in samples) of internal buffers

mc.line.name: mc.line
mc.line.description: Generate ramps and line segments from one value to another within a specified amount of time.

mc.line~.name: mc.line~
mc.line~.description: Use the line~ object to generate a signal ramp or envelope. It uses the Max time format syntax; envelope times can be either single valued fixed or tempo-relative.

mc.list~.name: mc.list~
mc.list~.digest: Create a multichannel signal from a list of values
mc.list~.description: Use mc.list~ to create a multichannel signal from a list of values. The number of output channels is determined by the number of arguments provided to the object.

mc.live.gain~.name: mc.live.gain~
mc.live.gain~.category: Live UI Objects, Live MSP Objects
mc.live.gain~.description: mc.live.gain~ is a slider that scales input audio signals and provides a visual indication of the current sound level on a deciBel scale.

inlets.digest: Audio Signal to be Scaled (ch 1), (Int/Float) Parameter Value (-70.00-6.00)

mc.log~.name: mc.log~
mc.log~.description: Use the log~ object to output a signal composed of the logarithms of its input values. You can specify the logarithmic base.

mc.lookup~.name: mc.lookup~
mc.lookup~.description: lookup~ allows you to use a table of samples (buffer~ object) to do waveshaping on a signal, in which the Y values of an input signal are used as X values to look up new signal values. Input values of -1 to +1 are mapped to table values between 0 (or the specified sample offset) and the size of the table.

mc.lores~.name: mc.lores~
mc.lores~.description: lores~ implements an inexpensive lowpass with an adjustment that lets you add a specified resonance. The middle inlet sets a kind of cutoff frequency, but the sharpness of the filter depends on the resonance passed in through the right inlet (0 is a little bit sharp and 1 as sharp as possible).

mc.loudness~.name: mc.loudness~
mc.loudness~.description: The loudness~ object reports the loudness of a signal according to the EBU R 128 standard. Momentary, short term, and integrated loudness measures are reported in LUFS (loudness units referenced to full scale). Additionally, the signal's loudness range is reported in loudness units, while sample peak true peak values are reported in dB. You can use these measurements to normalize audio signals for various broadcast and streaming platforms.

mc.makelist.name: mc.makelist
mc.makelist.digest: Create a list from non-signal output of MC objects
mc.makelist.description: The mc.makelist object creates a list of values whose size is determined by the highest voice number it receives in its right inlet or specified by the value of its voices attribute.

inlets.digest: Items to Assemble Into a List

mc.matrix~.name: mc.matrix~
mc.matrix~.description:
matrix~ is an array of signal connectors and mixers (adders). It can have any number of inlets and outlets. Signals entering at each inlet can be routed to one or more of the outlets, with a variable amount of gain. If an outlet is connected to more than one inlet, its output signal is the sum of the signals from the inlets.
		
		When the matrix~ object is created as mcs.matrix~ all of its signal inlets are combined into a single multichannel inlet and all of its signal outlets are combined into a single multichannel outlet. The behavior of mcs.matrix~ is otherwise identical to matrix~.

mc.maximum~.name: mc.maximum~
mc.maximum~.digest: Compare two signals, output the maximum  (multichannel)
mc.maximum~.description: maximum~ outputs a signal which is the maximum of two input signals.

inlets.digest: (signal) Signal to be compared with Right Inlet
(signal) Signal to be compared with Left Inlet

outlets.digest: (signal) Maximum of Left and Right Signals

mc.midiplayer~.name: mc.midiplayer~
mc.midiplayer~.digest: Generate MIDI Events from Audio Signals
mc.midiplayer~.description: The mc.midiplayer~ object generates midievent note-on and note-off messages from audio signals representing triggers, note numbers, and velocity values. Typically you'll connect mc.midiplayer~ to vst~ which allows sample-accurate MIDI events at audio rates (assuming the hosted plug-in supports it).

outlets.digest: MIDI Event Output Not for vst~

mc.miditarget.name: mc.miditarget
mc.miditarget.digest: Map MIDI / MPE Channels to MC Channels
mc.miditarget.description: The mc.miditarget object lets you use the channel number of incoming MIDI data (in the form of midievent messages produced by the midiparse object) to target individual instances of objects inside the MC wrapper. Normally MIDI channel 1 is mapped to MC channel 1, but mc.miditarget also has an MPE mode that maps MPE global MIDI channel 1 to MC channel 0 (targeting all instances) and MIDI channel 2 to MC channel 1. Objects that can work with messages from mc.miditarget include mc.vst~ and mc.sfizz~.

outlets.digest: setvalue Index Followed by midievent Message

mc.minimum~.name: mc.minimum~
mc.minimum~.digest: Compare two signals, output the minimum  (multichannel)
mc.minimum~.description: minimum~ outputs a signal which is the minimum of two input signals.

inlets.digest: (signal) Signal to be compared with Right Inlet
(signal) Signal to be compared with Left Inlet

outlets.digest: (signal) Minimum of Left and Right Signals

mc.minmax~.name: mc.minmax~
mc.minmax~.description: minmax~ computes the minimum and maximum values of an input signal and outputs signals which are the maximum signal and the minimum signal as well as outputs of the minimum and maximum floats.

mc.mixdown~.name: mc.mixdown~
mc.mixdown~.digest: Mix and pan a multichannel signal
mc.mixdown~.description: The mc.mixdown~ object mixes channels in a multichannel signal connected to its left inlet to a multichannel output. mc.mixdown~ can use a signal connected to its right inlet to pan the channels of its left (mix) input signal.

mc.mstosamps~.name: mc.mstosamps~
mc.mstosamps~.description: Use the mstosamps~ object to convert an incoming signal carrying a millisecond value and output a signal which converts those millisecond values to a number of samples (at the current sampling rate).

inlets.digest: Milliseconds In, signal To Use For Sampling Rate

outlets.digest: (signal) Samples At Input signal or Current Sampling Rate
(float) Samples At Input signal or Current Sampling Rate

mc.mtof~.name: mc.mtof~
mc.mtof~.digest: Convert a MIDI note number to frequency at signal rate (multichannel)
mc.mtof~.description: Use mtof~ to convert MIDI note numbers to frequency at signal rate

mc.noise~.name: mc.noise~
mc.noise~.description: Use the noise~ object to generate a signal consisting of uniformly distributed random white-noise with values between -1.0 and 1.0.

mc.normalize~.name: mc.normalize~
mc.normalize~.digest: Scale on the basis of maximum amplitude  (multichannel)
mc.normalize~.description: normalize~ performs real-time normalization of its input by multiplying each input sample value by a scaling factor - computed as the maximum output value (sent either as a signal or a float in the right inlet) over the maximum signal input value received thus far.

mc.noteallocator~.name: mc.noteallocator~
mc.noteallocator~.digest: Manage voice numbers for MIDI note events
mc.noteallocator~.description: The mc.noteallocator~ object assigns voice numbers for MIDI and MPE note events using an optional multichannel signal to determine voice busy state. It also maintains a voice busy map used by other MC objects to avoid unnecessary processing for channels that are not actively playing notes.

mc.number~.name: mc.number~
mc.number~.digest: Multichannel signal monitor and constant generator
mc.number~.description: Use the mc.number~ object to display or generate multichannel signal values.

outlets.digest: Entered or received float values as a multichannel signal

mc.omx.4band~.name: mc.omx.4band~
mc.omx.4band~.description: omx.4band~ delivers the signal-processing power of Octimax in a 4-band compressor.

mc.omx.5band~.name: mc.omx.5band~
mc.omx.5band~.description: omx.5band~ delivers the signal-processing power of Octimax in a 5-band compressor.

mc.omx.comp~.name: mc.omx.comp~
mc.omx.comp~.description: omx.comp~ is a fully-featured signal compressor with limiting, gating, sidechain, and dual-band options.

mc.omx.peaklim~.name: mc.omx.peaklim~
mc.omx.peaklim~.description: omx.peaklim~ is a peak-limiter which allows for the specified control of signal amplitude.

mc.onepole~.name: mc.onepole~
mc.onepole~.description: The onepole~ object implements the simplest of IIR filters, providing a 6dB per octave attenuation. This filter is very efficient and useful for gently rolling off harsh high end and for smoothing out control signals.

mc.op~.name: mc.op~
mc.op~.digest: Apply arithmetic operators to a multichannel signal
mc.op~.description: The mc.op~ object, similar to jit.planeop, performs one of several arithmetic operations combining each sample of a multichannel input to produce a single-channel output.

mc.out~.name: mc.out~
mc.out~.digest: Signal outputs for a patcher loaded by
mc.out~.description: The mc.out~ object sends signals to multiple outlets of its poly~ object (or multiple channels within the output of mcs.poly~). The number of signal outlets is determined by the chans attribute.  The maximum number of channels for any single mc.out~ object is 128.

arguments.name: starting-outlet-number
arguments.digest: Starting signal outlet number in a

mc.overdrive~.name: mc.overdrive~
mc.overdrive~.description: The overdrive~ object uses a waveshaping function to distort audio signals. It amplifies signals, limiting the maximum value of the signal to +/- 1. Values outside of this range are removed using "soft clipping" somewhat like that of an overdriven tube-based circuit.

mc.pack~.name: mc.pack~
mc.pack~.digest: Combine single inputs into a multichannel signal
mc.pack~.description: The mc.pack~ object combines inputs (signals, multichannel signals, floats) into a multi-channel signal. Unlike mc.combine~, mc.pack~ creates an output multichannel signal that contains exactly the number of channels as its number of inlets.

arguments.name: size
arguments.digest: The number of inlets and output channels

mc.pattern~.name: mc.pattern~
mc.pattern~.description: The mc.pattern~ object holds patterns -- sequences of signal values. Like seq~, playback is driven by a phasor, but unlike seq~, the output consists of signals on one or more audio channels. In addition to event recording, mc.pattern~ has an interface for generating events from Max messages or via algorithms.

outlets.digest: Pattern Data in Response to getcontent Message
Pattern Data in Response to getcontent Message

mc.peakamp~.name: mc.peakamp~
mc.peakamp~.digest: Report the maximum amplitude of a signal  (multichannel)
mc.peakamp~.description: Use the peakamp~ object to monitor an incoming signal and reports the absolute value of the peak amplitude of the signal it has received since the last time it was reported.

mc.peek~.name: mc.peek~
mc.peek~.description: Use peek~ to read and write sample values to a named buffer~. Unlike related objects index~ and poke~, values and indices are specified as Max messages, and the object will function even when the audio is not turned on.

inlets.digest: Sample Value For Writing Into buffer~

mc.pfft~.name: mc.pfft~
mc.pfft~.description: The pfft~ object is designed to simplify spectral audio processing using the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT). In addition to performing the FFT and the Inverse Fast Fourier Transform (IFFT), pfft~ (with the help of its companion fftin~ and fftout~ objects) manages the necessary signal windowing, overlapping and adding needed to create a real-time Short Term Fourier Transform (STFT) analysis/resynthesis system.

mc.phasegroove~.name: mc.phasegroove~
mc.phasegroove~.description: The phasegroove~ object converts ramps between 0 and 1 into a sample-increment signal that you can use to control the groove~ object. phasegroove~ must be connected to a groove~. Once connected, it knows about the groove~ object's loop points and the sample it is playing.

mc.phaseshift~.name: mc.phaseshift~
mc.phaseshift~.digest: Distort the phase of a signal (multichannel)
mc.phaseshift~.description: The phaseshift~ object is a 2nd-order allpass filter.

arguments.name: frequency
arguments.digest: Center frequency of the phase transition band

arguments.name: q
arguments.digest: Q (bandwidth) of the phase transition band

mc.phasewrap~.name: mc.phasewrap~
mc.phasewrap~.digest: Wrap a signal between π and -π  (multichannel)
mc.phasewrap~.description: phasewrap~ takes any input signal and wrap it between π and -π values.

mc.phasor~.name: mc.phasor~
mc.phasor~.description: Use the phasor~ object to generate sawtooth waves suitable for sample-accurate control and timing tasks. For smoother sounding sawtooth generation, use the bandlimited saw~ object instead. The ramp rate can be set by frequency (Hz), or as an interval using the tempo-relative Max time format syntax.

mc.pink~.name: mc.pink~
mc.pink~.description: pink~ generates pink noise, as distinguished from white noise (which the MSP object noise~ generates). White noise has constant spectral power per hertz of bandwidth, while pink noise has constant power per octave. Subjectively, pink noise sounds less hissy than white noise.

mc.pitchshift~.name: mc.pitchshift~
mc.pitchshift~.description: Use the pitchshift~ object to load a perform pitch-shifting on an input signal.

mc.playlist~.name: mc.playlist~
mc.playlist~.digest: Play sound files with multichannel output
mc.playlist~.description: Use mc.playlist~ to organize sound files and play them back using multichannel outputs. Each file's waveform is shown in a clip where you can select a portion of the file for playback. Drag clips within an mc.playlist~ to re-order them, or drag clips to other mc.playlist~ objects by using the dotted handle on the clip's left edge.

mc.play~.name: mc.play~
mc.play~.description:
Use the play~ object as a playback interface for a buffer~. that plays back samples based on an offset within the buffer. It is typically used with the line~ object, but can be used with any signal that generates a changing position value in milliseconds. The groove~ object provides another option for sample playback.
		
		When the play~ object is created as mcs.play~ all of its signal outlets are combined into a single multichannel outlet. The behavior of mcs.play~ is otherwise identical to play~.

inlets.digest: Sample Time to Play in ms

outlets.digest: bang When playback reaches destination or when playback is stopped with a

mc.poltocar~.name: mc.poltocar~
mc.poltocar~.digest: Signal Polar to Cartesian coordinate conversion  (multichannel)
mc.poltocar~.description: poltocar~ will take any given signal as a polar coordinate and output the cartesian conversion of that signal.

mc.poly~.name: mc.poly~
mc.poly~.description: Use the mc.poly~ to encapsulate a patcher inside an object box, to specify the patcher filename and the number of instances you want to load as arguments to the poly~ object, and to control object processing and routing in the loaded patcher instances.

arguments.name: patcher-name
arguments.digest: Name of a patcher to be loaded

arguments.name: number-of-instances
arguments.digest: Number of patcher instances to be loaded

mc.pong~.name: mc.pong~
mc.pong~.description: Use the pong~ object to clip, fold, or wrap its input within the range of a low value and a high value.

arguments.name: folding-mode
arguments.digest: Set fold, wrap, or clip mode.

mc.pow~.name: mc.pow~
mc.pow~.description: pow~ raises the base value (set in the right inlet) to the power of the exponent (set in the left inlet). Either inlet can receive a signal, float or int.

mc.rampsmooth~.name: mc.rampsmooth~
mc.rampsmooth~.description: Smooths an incoming signal across n samples. Each time an incoming value changes, it begins a linear ramp to reach this value.

mc.ramp~.name: mc.ramp~
mc.ramp~.digest: Trigger a Single Ramp With an Audio Signal (multichannel)
mc.ramp~.description: The ramp~ object generates a single signal ramp between a start and end value when it detects a change in an audio signal connected to its left inlet. The duration of the ramp can be a fixed value or based on the signal in ramp~'s second inlet.

mc.rand~.name: mc.rand~
mc.rand~.description: Use the rand~ object to generate a signal consisting of random values between -1 and 1 generated at a frequency specified by its input. It interpolates linearly between these values.

mc.range~.name: mc.range~
mc.range~.digest: Generate a multichannel signal with a range of constant values
mc.range~.description: The mc.range~ object generates a multichannel signal filled with constant values distributed across a defined range.

mc.rate~.name: mc.rate~
mc.rate~.digest: Time-scale the output of a phasor~  (multichannel)
mc.rate~.description: The rate~ object accepts an input signal from a phasor~ and time scales it by a multiplier received as a float in its right inlet. Numbers less than 1 create several ramps per phase cycle. Numbers greater than 1 create fewer ramps. This can be useful for implementing a phasor~-synchronized system in MSP.

mc.record~.name: mc.record~
mc.record~.description: record~ records a signal into a buffer~ object. You can specify how many channels to record with the optional input-channels argument. Recording stops when the buffer~ is filled.

mc.rect~.name: mc.rect~
mc.rect~.description: Use the rect~ object to generate an anti-aliased rectangle wave.

mc.resize~.name: mc.resize~
mc.resize~.digest: Resize a multichannel signal using selected channels
mc.resize~.description: The mc.resize~ object changes the number of channels in a connected single- or multichannel signal, producing an output multichannel signal with a number of channels specified by its argument. Optionally, the output can consist of selected channels of the input, or, when increasing the number of channels, the additional output channels can replicate the existing inputs.

mc.reson~.name: mc.reson~
mc.reson~.description: Use the reson~ object when you need a resonant bandpass filter.

outlets.digest: closest note and deviation in cents

mc.round~.name: mc.round~
mc.round~.description: Use the round~ object to round a signal input to an integer multiple.

mc.route.name: mc.route
mc.route.digest: Direct output of messages based on an index received
mc.route.description: The mc.route objects works with the non-signal output of objects such as mc.snapshot~, mc.peakamp~, poly~ and mc.poly~ (when non-signal outputs are used). These objects precede non-signal output with a voice index identifying the channel or patcher instance that produced the value. mc.route can use this index to direct the value to an outlet.

arguments.name: channel-count
arguments.digest: Sets the number of channels to receive from

mc.sah~.name: mc.sah~
mc.sah~.description: Use sah~ to capture ("sample") and output ("hold") a value from an input signal whenever a trigger signal satisfies a threshold value according to one of several trigger conditions. The default trigger mode (Ascending) requires the signal to go from being at or below the threshold value to above it. At the moment the trigger condition is satisfied, the input signal is sampled and "held" until the trigger condition is satisfied again, at which point the input is sampled again. Generally this allows one signal to be synchronized to the behavior of another.

inlets.digest: Values To Sample, double Sets Trigger Threshold

mc.sampstoms~.name: mc.sampstoms~
mc.sampstoms~.digest: Convert time from samples to milliseconds  (multichannel)
mc.sampstoms~.description: Use the sampstoms~ object to convert an incoming signal carrying a time value in samples and output a signal which converts that number of samples to a number of milliseconds (at the current sampling rate).

inlets.digest: Number of Samples In, signal to Use For Sampling Rate

outlets.digest: (signal) Milliseconds At Input signal or Current Sampling Rate
(float) Milliseconds At Input signal or Current Sampling Rate

mc.sash~.name: mc.sash~
mc.sash~.description: The sash~ object performs sample-and-hold operations on an input signal but unlike the sah~ includes a buffer (the extra "s" in sash~ is for "storage") that can holds samples of the input. Sample capture and sample output are triggered independently of each other, and sash~ includes a variety of methods for selecting the output sample from the memory buffer.

mc.saw~.name: mc.saw~
mc.saw~.description: Use the saw~ object to generate an anti-aliased sawtooth wave.

mc.scale~.name: mc.scale~
mc.scale~.digest: Map an input range of signal values to an output range  (multichannel)
mc.scale~.description: Use the scale~ object to map an input range of signal values to an output range. The ranges can be specified with hi and lo reversed for inverted mapping. If specified, the mapping can also be exponential.

mc.selector~.name: mc.selector~
mc.selector~.digest: Assign one of several inputs to an outlet (multichannel)
mc.selector~.description:
Use the selector~ object to choose between one of several input signals (or none). The selector~ is similar to the Max switch object but for signals, however if no input is chosen, it outputs a signal composed of zero values.
		
		When the selector~ object is created as mcs.selector~ all of its signal inlets are combined into a single multichannel inlet. The behavior of mcs.selector~ is otherwise identical to selector~ with the exception that mcs.selector~ changes its number of inputs (auto-adapts) as the number of inputs in the multi-channel signal connected to its right inlet change.

inlets.digest: int/signal Turns Input Off or Routes to Output

mc.separate~.name: mc.separate~
mc.separate~.description: The mc.separate~ object splits a multichannel signal into two or more multichannel signals with specific channel counts.

mc.seq~.name: mc.seq~
mc.seq~.description: The seq~ object is an event sequencer that is driven by a signal input. seq~ can be used to create looping sequences of control data that are synchronized to a phasor~.

inlets.digest: phasor~ input, commands, messages to be sequenced

outlets.digest: id on read, (bang) when done reading

mc.sfizz~.name: mc.sfizz~
mc.sfizz~.description:
sfizz~ is a player for files in the Sfz format. The format uses simple text files that specify how audio sample files are triggered by MIDI notes and controllers. (Information on the format is available at
		
			sfzformat.com
		
		.) The object accepts MIDI messages and produces two or more audio outputs. sfizz~ makes use of the open source
		
			Sfizz library project
		
		that supports a
		
			large subset of standard Sfz opcodes

mc.sfplay~.name: mc.sfplay~
mc.sfplay~.description: Use the mc.sfplay~ object to play audio files from disk. Supported formats include AIFF, WAVE, MP3, OGG, FLAC, M4A, CAF, WF64, WAVE64, and Raw Data.

inlets.digest: open Opens File, int Plays Cue, 0 Stops

arguments.name: position-outlet-flag
arguments.digest: Create an outlet that reports positioning information

arguments.name: object-reference-name
arguments.digest: Name to identify the object and its contents

mc.sfrecord~.name: mc.sfrecord~
mc.sfrecord~.digest: Record to audio file on disk (multi-channel)
mc.sfrecord~.description: Use the mc.sfrecord~ object to record and save multi-channel MSP output as an audio file.

mc.shape~.name: mc.shape~
mc.shape~.description: An envelope for the shape~ object can be defined using the same format as the line~ or zigzag~ objects (and edited graphically by connecting a function object). Unlike line~ the time values of shape~ are normalized so that the output occurs within signal ramp of a phasor from 0 to 1 (or 1 to 0). This means you can trigger the output sample-accurately and determine the time of the function by using different ramp durations (or phasor~ frequencies).

mc.sig~.name: mc.sig~
mc.sig~.description: Use the sig~ object to convert regular numbers into audio signals.

mc.sinh~.name: mc.sinh~
mc.sinh~.description: Use the sinh~ object to calculate and output a signal that is the hyperbolic sine function of each sample of the input signal.

mc.sinx~.name: mc.sinx~
mc.sinx~.description: sinx~ is a real sine function whose input expects values between 0 and 2π (similar to cosx~ but unlike cos~ which is designed to work with phasor~'s output from 0 to 1).

mc.slide~.name: mc.slide~
mc.slide~.description: Use the slide~ object for envelope following and lowpass filtering.

mc.snapshot~.name: mc.snapshot~
mc.snapshot~.description: Use the snapshot~ object to convert a signal value into a float message when a bang is received or according to a specified interval. The interval can be set to a fixed or tempo-relative rate using the Max time format syntax.

mc.snowfall~.name: mc.snowfall~
mc.snowfall~.description: The snowfall~ object controls a multi-dimensional particle as one or more audio ramps whose lifetime is triggered and controlled by an input phasor ramp from 0 to 1. The object will typically be used within a larger system of quasi-independent particles using the MC Wrapper as mc.snowfall~.

mc.snowphasor~.name: mc.snowphasor~
mc.snowphasor~.description: The mc.snowphasor~ object generates phasor ramps in multiple audio channels according to global controls such as an average rate. The object can also trigger ramps from a multi-channel audio signal.

mc.spike~.name: mc.spike~
mc.spike~.digest: Report intervals of zero to non-zero transitions  (multichannel)
mc.spike~.description: spike~ reports intervals of zero to non-zero transitions.

outlets.digest: (float) Outputs interval on zero to non-zero transition

mc.sqrt~.name: mc.sqrt~
mc.sqrt~.description: sqrt~ will output a signal which is the square-root function of the input signal.

mc.stash~.name: mc.stash~
mc.stash~.digest: Store and Recall Audio Signal Values (multichannel)
mc.stash~.description: Use the stash~ object to store and recall a buffer of signal values. In one mode, stash~ can perform sample-and-hold operations on an input signal but unlike the sah~, stash~ reads from a buffer rather than holds a single sample. stash~ can be a simple interpolating lookup table or audio-rate step sequencer.

mc.stepdiv~.name: mc.stepdiv~
mc.stepdiv~.digest: Generate Phasors for Each Step of a Function (multichannel)
mc.stepdiv~.description: The stepdiv~ object produces a signal output consisting of a sequence of phasors according to the delta times in a breakpoint function list produced by the function object. When a phasor signal is connected to the left inlet, stepdiv~ subdivides the phasor according to the relative times of the breakpoints. When no signal input is connected, stepdiv~ produces phasors over the specified delta times. Connect the left and right outputs of stepdiv~ to stepfun~ to generate the actual function data.

mc.stepfun~.name: mc.stepfun~
mc.stepfun~.digest: Generate a Function Sequenced by Input Phasors (multichannel)
mc.stepfun~.description: The stepfun~ object generates a breakpoint function whose timing is determined by a seqeuence of input phasor ramps. If the input ramps are non-linear, the output of stepfun~ will be similarly non-linear.

mc.stutter~.name: mc.stutter~
mc.stutter~.digest: Signal capture buffer for granular playback  (multichannel)
mc.stutter~.description: The stutter~ object keeps a history of its signal input (left inlet). Upon receiving an int (left inlet), it copies that number of the most recently received samples to another playback buffer. This buffer may be cycled through by its phase, 0-1 (right inlet). On receiving a bang (left inlet) or a trigger signal (middle inlet), the last integer number of samples are copied to the playback buffer.

arguments.name: number-of-copied-samples
arguments.digest: Number of samples copied from record to playback

mc.subdiv~.name: mc.subdiv~
mc.subdiv~.description: The subdiv~ object evenly subdivides an input phasor signal into any integer number of output phasor signals.

mc.svf~.name: mc.svf~
mc.svf~.description: The svf~ object is an implementation of a state-variable filter algorithm described in Hal Chamberlin's book, "Musical Applications of Microprocessors." A unique feature of this filter object is that it produces lowpass, highpass, bandpass, and bandreject (notch) output simultaneously - all four are available as outlets.

arguments.name: radians
arguments.digest: Set frequency input mode in radians

mc.swing~.name: mc.swing~
mc.swing~.digest: Subdivide a phasor into two unequal phasors (multichannel)
mc.swing~.description: The swing~ object outputs two phasor signals in the space of one input phasor, with the ability to adjust the "swing" or proportion of the time of the input phasor given to the first of the two output phasors. If the swing is set to 0.5, both the first and second output phasors are equal. But if the swing is set to 0.75, the first phasor lasts for three fourths of the input and the second phasor lasts for one fourth of the time.

mc.sync~.name: mc.sync~
mc.sync~.digest: Synchronize MSP with an external source  (multichannel)
mc.sync~.description: sync~ outputs a 0-1 ramp, like phasor~. The frequency of the ramp can be specified in beats per minute (BPM), or by sending sync~ a tempo reference via tap tempo, MIDI beat clock, or an audio signal containing a 'click track.' Also, sync~ generates MIDI beat clock to synchronize external devices.

inlets.digest: synchronization input and messages to sync~

arguments.name: channel-count
arguments.digest: Sets the number of channels to send to

mc.table~.name: mc.table~
mc.table~.description: Use table~ to remap incoming signal values with a table object.

mc.tanh~.name: mc.tanh~
mc.tanh~.description: Use the tanh~ object to calculate and output a signal that is the hyperbolic tangent function of each sample of the input signal.

mc.tanx~.name: mc.tanx~
mc.tanx~.description: Use the tanx~ object to calculate and output a signal that is the tangent function of each sample of the input signal. The tanx~ object is a true π based function - it varies from the tanh~ object, whose output is based around 1 and is intended for use as a lookup table with the phasor~ object.

mc.tapin~.name: mc.tapin~
mc.tapin~.description: mc.tapin~ receives a multichannel in and copies into a delay line. Using mc.tapout~, you can read from the delay line at various delay times. You must connect the outlet of an mc.tapin~ object to the mc.tapout~ object you want to use with the delay line. Note that this is not a signal connection, since no signal travels between the objects. It is merely a way to indicate that the objects share the same delay memory.

outlets.digest: Connect To One Or More mc.tapout~ Objects

mc.tapout~.name: mc.tapout~
mc.tapout~.description:
The outlet of an mc.tapin~ object must be connected to the left inlet of mc.tapout~ in order for the delay line to function.
		
		
		The mc.tapout~ object has one or more inlets and one or more outlets. A delay time signal or number received in an inlet affects the output signal coming out of the outlet directly below the inlet.

inlets.digest: (signal/float) Delay Time in ms. Signal-based delay uses interpolation, which introduces a one-sample delay

mc.target.name: mc.target
mc.target.digest: Format messages to control MC objects
mc.target.description: The mc.target object simpifies creating the setvalue messages used to target specific instances within MC wrapper objects.

mc.targetlist.name: mc.targetlist
mc.targetlist.digest: Format messages based on inlet number for controlling MC objects
mc.targetlist.description: The mc.targetlist object simplifies creating the setvalue messages used to target specific instances within MC wrapper objects. The object maps the index of an inlet where a message was received to a voice index used in the resulting setvalue message.

mc.teeth~.name: mc.teeth~
mc.teeth~.digest: Comb filter with feedforward and feedback delay control  (multichannel)
mc.teeth~.description: The teeth~ object implements a comb filter, in which a slightly delayed version of a signal is added to itself, causing phase cancellations and a spectrum that looks like a comb.

mc.thresh~.name: mc.thresh~
mc.thresh~.digest: Detect signal above a set level  (multichannel)
mc.thresh~.description: Use the thresh~ object to detect signals which exceed a specified level.

arguments.name: low/reset-threshold
arguments.digest: Set the reset or low threshold level

arguments.name: high/set-threshold
arguments.digest: Set the set or high threshold level

mc.train~.name: mc.train~
mc.train~.description: train~ generates a pulse signal whose period is specifiable in terms of milliseconds (see <~ for a method of making a pulse wave at a specified frequency). It also sends out a bang when going from 0 to 1, so it can be used as a metronome with a floating-point interval.

mc.transpose~.name: mc.transpose~
mc.transpose~.description: The mc.transpose~ object groups one or more multichannel signal inputs into multichannel signal outputs where channels in the outputs share the same channel index in the inputs.

mc.trapezoid~.name: mc.trapezoid~
mc.trapezoid~.description: trapezoid~ is a trapezoidal wavetable with signal inputs to change ramp up/down phase position. The default lo/hi points are 0. and 1., but may be changed using the lo/hi messages.

mc.triangle~.name: mc.triangle~
mc.triangle~.description: triangle~ is a triangle/ramp wavetable with signal input to change phase offset of the peak value. The default lo/hi points are -1. and 1., but may be changed using the lo/hi messages.

mc.tri~.name: mc.tri~
mc.tri~.description: tri~ generates a triangle wave whose component frequencies are resistant to aliasing.

mc.trunc~.name: mc.trunc~
mc.trunc~.description: trunc~ converts signals with values such as 1.75 to 1.0. Negative values are modified so that -1.75 becomes -1.0. This object is very simple but computationally expensive.

mc.twist~.name: mc.twist~
mc.twist~.description: The twist~ object accepts a ramp between 0 and 1 (such as one generated by phasor~) and produces a piecewise linear approximation of an expoential function using the same algorithm as the curve~ object. The curvature of the ramp is controlled by the curve attribute that ranges from -1 to 1. A curve value of 0 produces an output ramp identical to the input.

mc.unpack~.name: mc.unpack~
mc.unpack~.digest: Split a multichannel signal into single-channel signals
mc.unpack~.description: The mc.unpack~ object splits a multichannel signal connected to its inlet into single-channel signals. The number of single-channel outputs is specified by its argument.

mc.updown~.name: mc.updown~
mc.updown~.digest: Trapezoidal Function Generator With Constant Attack and Release (multichannel)
mc.updown~.description:
The updown~ object is driven by a ramp from 0 - 1 or 1 - 0. It generates a trapezoidal function where the attack and release times are independent of the speed of the input ramp, unlike trapezoid~ where start and end times are specified as a proportion of the total input ramp cycle and thus will vary with the speed of the input ramp.
		
		
		Note that the up phase of the updown~ object's output occurs after the reset of a phasor or a zero-to-non-zero transition, whether the input ramp moves up or down. The down phase of the output occurs in the time before a reset (or the end of the ramp) is expected to occur.

mc.vectral~.name: mc.vectral~
mc.vectral~.description: Use the vectral~ object to filter frame-based signal data such as the output of the fft~ object.

mc.voiceallocator~.name: mc.voiceallocator~
mc.voiceallocator~.description: The mc.voiceallocator~ object assigns voice numbers to incoming messages using an optional multichannel signal to determine voice busy state. It also maintains a voice busy map used by other MC objects to avoid unnecessary processing for channels that are not actively producing audio in response to events.

inlets.digest: Multichannel signal representing busy state; any message along with specific messages to manage allocating and releasing voices.

mc.vst~.name: mc.vst~
mc.vst~.digest: Host VST, VST3 and Audio Unit plug-ins (multichannel)
mc.vst~.description: Use the vst~ object to load a real-time VST, VST3 or Audio Unit plug-in and use its audio processing in MSP. When vst~ is instantiated as mcs.vst~, the plug-in's audio inputs are combined into a single multichannel inlet and its audio outputs are combined into a single multichannel outlet.

outlets.digest: Parameter Index and Value When Changed

mc.wave~.name: mc.wave~
mc.wave~.description: wave~ reads from a portion of a buffer~ to produce a repeating waveform, given a signal input that goes between 0 and 1 (for example, using a phasor~) to define the position in the buffer. When the wave~ object is instantiated as mcs.wave~ its outputs are combined into a single multichannel output but otherwise it functions identically to wave~.

mc.what~.name: mc.what~
mc.what~.digest: Generate Impulses for a List of Audio Values (multichannel)
mc.what~.description: The what~ object generates an impulse -- a signal value of 1 for one sample -- when its input signal crosses a value threshold. The object accepts a list of threshold values and will generate impulses for all of them. Connect a phasor~ to what~ to create a repeating rhythm. For example, when sending in a list of 0 0.25 0.5 and 0.75, what~ will output an impulse four times for each phasor~ ramp.

arguments.name: values
arguments.digest: One or more values to detect

mc.where~.name: mc.where~
mc.where~.digest: Report Elapsed and Remaining Time of a Phasor (multichannel)
mc.where~.description: The where~ object generates two signals: one reports the elapsed time since the reset of a phasor ramp; the other predicts of the remaining time until the phasor resets again. You can use these time values to synchronize events to specific times within a ramp instead of its relative phase. For example, if you want something to happen at 30 milliseconds before the phasor is scheduled to reset, you can wait until the remaining time signal has a value of 30 or less. where~ has a direction detection feature so it can report the elapsed and remaining time of ramps that go from 1 to 0 (as produced by a phasor~ object with negative frequency) in addition to ramps that go from 0 to 1.

mc.zerox~.name: mc.zerox~
mc.zerox~.description: zerox~ functions as a zero-crossing counter or transient detector.

outlets.digest: (signal) Number of Zero-Crossings per Signal Vector

mc.zigzag~.name: mc.zigzag~
mc.zigzag~.description: Use zigzag~ to generate multisegment linear ramps. This object is similar to line~, but retains information about the ramp after it has been output, and allows modification of the list values for the ramp.
